understood_request: For each location, identify which products have a qty_on_hand (at that specific location) below their reorder_point. The reorder_point is a product-level attribute. The comparison must be made per location, preserving the product-location grain from the source inventory_levels table — not summed across locations.

mart.ddl: CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS mart_products_below_reorder_by_location (location_bk VARCHAR, location_name VARCHAR, product_bk VARCHAR, product_name VARCHAR, sku VARCHAR, reorder_point BIGINT, safety_stock BIGINT, qty_on_hand BIGINT, reorder_shortfall BIGINT, mart_insert_dts TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP)
mart.populate_sql: DELETE FROM mart_products_below_reorder_by_location; INSERT INTO mart_products_below_reorder_by_location SELECT hl.location_bk, sld.location_name, hp.product_bk, spd.product_name, spd.sku, spd.reorder_point, spd.safety_stock, sil.qty_on_hand, spd.reorder_point - sil.qty_on_hand AS reorder_shortfall, CURRENT_TIMESTAMP AS mart_insert_dts FROM lnk_product_location lpl INNER JOIN sat_inventory_levels sil ON lpl.product_location_hk = sil.product_location_hk INNER JOIN hub_product hp ON lpl.product_hk = hp.product_hk INNER JOIN sat_product_details spd ON hp.product_hk = spd.product_hk INNER JOIN hub_location hl ON lpl.location_hk = hl.location_hk INNER JOIN sat_location_details sld ON hl.location_hk = sld.location_hk WHERE sil.load_end_dts IS NULL AND spd.load_end_dts IS NULL AND sld.load_end_dts IS NULL AND sil.qty_on_hand < spd.reorder_point

join_sequence.filter: sld.load_end_dts IS NULL AND sil.qty_on_hand < spd.reorder_point
